events.apidestination.CfnAbout:
Creates an API destination, which is an HTTP invocation endpoint configured as a target for events.

When using ApiDesinations with OAuth authentication we recommend these best practices:
+ Create a secret in Secrets Manager with your OAuth credentials.
+ Reference that secret in your CloudFormation template for `AWS::Events::Connection` using CloudFormation dynamic reference syntax. For more information, see [Secrets Manager secrets](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/dynamic-references.html

events.apidestination.ConnectionArn.Description: The ARN of the connection to use for the API destination. The destination endpoint must support the authorization type specified for the connection.

events.apidestination.Description.Description: A description for the API destination to create.

events.apidestination.HttpMethod.Description: The method to use for the request to the HTTP invocation endpoint.
events.apidestination.HttpMethod.Allowed values: DELETE | GET | HEAD | OPTIONS | PATCH | POST | PUT

events.apidestination.InvocationEndpoint.Description: The URL to the HTTP invocation endpoint for the API destination.

events.apidestination.InvocationRateLimi.Description: The maximum number of requests per second to send to the HTTP invocation endpoint.

events.apidestination.Name.Description: The name for the API destination to create.
